depression.description: I often feel that my family is judging me, especially during gatherings. It makes me anxious and I want to avoid them altogether.
I constantly feel like my achievements don't matter. Even when I do something good, I can't shake off the feeling that it's not enough.
I find it really hard to share my successes with my family because I fear they will belittle me or not understand. It makes me feel ashamed.

Delusion.topic: Belief that family interactions lead to discomfort
Delusion.description: I believe that any interaction with my family will lead to discomfort and judgment. This thought makes me want to isolate myself.
Sometimes I find myself pushing people away or causing emotional hurt to see if they care. It's like I need to validate my own significance.

psychosis.description: I feel this deep sense of disconnection from my family, especially my parents. It's as if I'm living in a different world from them.

psychosis.topic: Struggles with self-worth based on family approval
psychosis.description: I often think that I need my parents' acknowledgment to feel worthy. When I don't get it, I feel completely worthless.
